 {"id":"bd-1gl","title":"E2E extension runtime (WASM+JS) with log schema + artifacts","description":"# Goal
Create an end-to-end extension runtime run (WASM + JS tiers) with deterministic artifacts and logs.

# Scope
- Execute the pinned sample set (`docs/extension-sample.json`) across both WASM and JS tiers where applicable.
- Ensure extc pipeline + compat scanner are used (no manual source edits).
- Exercise hostcalls, tool calls, event hooks, and UI hooks where supported.
- Validate both success and expected-deny cases (capability policy).

# Logging / Artifacts
- Emit JSONL logs per bd-4u9: scenario id, extension id, tier, policy mode, timings, and redaction summary.
- Capture artifacts: compat ledger, hostcall audit log, trace viewer output, stdout/stderr, and any produced files.
- Provide a deterministic artifact index and hash list.

# Tests
- E2E script (CLI or harness) with offline deterministic mode.
- Assertions for: exit status, expected outputs, and policy denials.

# Dependencies
- Runtime wiring (bd-2i5), smoke suite (bd-2ni), logging spec (bd-4u9), compat pipeline (bd-xgo).

# Acceptance Criteria
- Full pinned sample set runs with no manual edits; failures are explained with structured logs.
- Artifacts are reproducible and sufficient for diff-based debugging.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test

 {"id":"bd-1hkm","title":"Task: Implement Tool Hostcall Handler (pi.tool)","description":"# Task: Implement Tool Hostcall Handler

## Objective

Implement the handler for HostcallKind::Tool that routes pi.tool() calls from extensions to the Rust ToolRegistry.

## Background

When an extension calls pi.tool("read", {path: "foo.txt"}), the flow is:
1. JS: pi.tool() enqueues HostcallRequest with kind=Tool
2. Rust: Dispatcher drains request, extracts tool name + params
3. Rust: Looks up tool in ToolRegistry, calls execute()
4. Rust: Converts ToolOutput to HostcallOutcome
5. JS: Promise resolves with result

## Implementation

```rust
impl ExtensionDispatcher {
    /// Handle pi.tool(name, input) hostcalls.
    /// 
    /// This allows extensions to invoke any tool in the registry,
    /// including built-in tools and other extension-registered tools.
    async fn dispatch_tool(
        &self,
        name: &str,
        input: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> HostcallOutcome {
        // Find tool in registry
        let Some(tool) = self.tool_registry.get(name) else {
            return HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "TOOL_NOT_FOUND".to_string(),
                message: format!("Tool '{}' not found in registry", name),
            };
        };
        
        // Generate unique tool_call_id for tracking
        let tool_call_id = format!("ext-{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string()[..8].to_string());
        
        // Execute the tool
        match tool.execute(&tool_call_id, input, None).await {
            Ok(output) => {
                // Convert ToolOutput to JSON Value
                let result = serde_json::json!({
                    "content": output.content,
                    "details": output.details,
                });
                HostcallOutcome::Success(result)
            }
            Err(e) => HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "TOOL_EXECUTION_ERROR".to_string(),
                message: e.to_string(),
            },
        }
    }
}
```

## Error Handling

| Error Condition | Code | Behavior |
|-----------------|------|----------|
| Tool not found | TOOL_NOT_FOUND | Return error outcome |
| Tool execution fails | TOOL_EXECUTION_ERROR | Return error with message |
| Invalid params | INVALID_PARAMS | Return validation error |
| Timeout | TIMEOUT | Return timeout error |

## Integration with Update Callbacks

The TypeScript reference supports streaming tool updates via the onUpdate callback. For v1, we can omit this (return final result only). Future enhancement:

```rust
// Future: Support streaming updates
let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel(16);
let update_callback = Some(Box::new(move |update| {
    let _ = tx.try_send(update);
}));
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: Tool found and executed successfully
2. Unit test: Tool not found returns error
3. Unit test: Tool execution error propagates
4. Integration test: Built-in tool (read) via extension
5. Integration test: Extension-registered tool via another extension

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-389q (ExtensionDispatcher struct)
- Blocks: End-to-end extension tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] dispatch_tool() method implemented
- [ ] Tool lookup in registry works
- [ ] Successful execution returns Success outcome
- [ ] Errors return Error outcome with code + message
- [ ] Tool call IDs are unique
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:40.871031814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:51:28.199266466Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:51:28.199200443Z","close_reason":"Implemented ExtensionDispatcher dispatch_and_complete() for HostcallKind::Tool using ToolRegistry; added unit tests covering success + unknown tool rejection; gates green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hkm","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:40.885917174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hkm","depends_on_id":"bd-389q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:48.505416010Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1i5x","title":"Implement pi.sendMessage() and pi.sendUserMessage() for message injection","description":"Allow extensions to inject messages into the conversation.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
// Custom message (not for LLM, optional display)
pi.sendMessage({
  customType: string,
  content: string,
  display?: boolean,
  details?: unknown
}, {
  triggerTurn?: boolean,
  deliverAs?: 'steer' | 'followUp' | 'nextTurn'
});

// User message (triggers LLM turn)
pi.sendUserMessage(content, {
  deliverAs?: 'steer' | 'followUp'
});
```

## Delivery Modes
- **steer**: Interrupt after current tool, skip remaining tool calls
- **followUp**: Queue after agent finishes current turn
- **nextTurn**: Default, processed as next user input

## Implementation Requirements
1. Hostcall handlers for 'sendMessage' and 'sendUserMessage'
2. Integration with agent message queues (steering_queue, follow_up_queue)
3. Custom message type in session entries
4. Display logic in TUI for custom messages

## Data Flow
1. Extension calls pi.sendMessage({customType: 'bookmark', content: 'Saved\!'})
2. Hostcall creates CustomMessage entry
3. If display=true, shows in TUI
4. If triggerTurn=true, queues for processing

## Test Plan
- Unit test: sendMessage creates correct entry type
- Unit test: sendUserMessage triggers agent turn
- Integration test: steer mode interrupts current processing
- Integration test: followUp mode queues correctly

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (hostcall handlers)
- src/extensions_js.rs (bridge to Rust)
- src/agent.rs (message queue integration)
- src/interactive.rs (display custom messages)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:41.477300174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T03:51:39.501381380Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i5x","depends_on_id":"bd-2ca4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:41.494460870Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i5x","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:35.499164360Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ilq","title":"Implement pi.registerShortcut() for extension keyboard bindings","description":"Allow extensions to register custom keyboard shortcuts.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
pi.registerShortcut(key, {
  description: string,
  handler: (ctx) => Promise<void>
});
```

Key format: 'ctrl+u', 'alt+shift+x', 'f1', etc.

## Implementation Requirements
1. ShortcutRegistry in extension runtime
2. Hostcall handler for 'registerShortcut'
3. Integration with keybindings system (src/keybindings.rs)
4. Validation that key isn't reserved (interrupt, clear, exit, suspend)
5. Shortcut execution via extension runtime

## Reserved Keys (cannot be overridden)
- ctrl+c (interrupt)
- ctrl+d (exit)
- ctrl+l (clear)
- ctrl+z (suspend)

## Data Flow
1. Extension calls pi.registerShortcut('ctrl+u', {...})
2. Hostcall validates key not reserved, stores in registry
3. KeyMsg in bubbletea checked against extension shortcuts
4. If match, invoke JS handler with ExtensionContext

## Test Plan
- Unit test: registerShortcut stores shortcut
- Unit test: reserved keys rejected
- Integration test: registered shortcut triggers handler

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (ShortcutRegistry)
- src/extensions_js.rs (hostcall handler)
- src/keybindings.rs (integrate with extension shortcuts)
- src/interactive.rs (dispatch to extension handler on key)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:48.738747397Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:32.254817020Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ilq","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:48.745921073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ilq","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:32.254783057Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1iwi","title":"Workstream: Interactive Editor UX Parity (autocomplete, multiline, bang, images)","description":"# Goal
Bring the **interactive editor experience** to parity with legacy pi-mono, focusing on:
- Autocomplete (commands, templates, skills, file references, path completion)
- Multi-line editing behavior (Shift+Enter inserts newline, Enter submits)
- Bash shortcuts (`!` and `!!`)
- Clipboard image paste + drag/drop attachments

# Legacy Behavior (Source-of-Truth)
From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/README.md` → “Editor”:
- File reference: type `@` to fuzzy-search project files
- Path completion: Tab to complete paths
- Multi-line: Shift+Enter (Ctrl+Enter on Windows Terminal)
- Images: Ctrl+V to paste, or drag onto terminal
- Bash commands: `!command` runs and sends output to LLM, `!!command` runs without sending

Legacy implementation details:
- Autocomplete is provided by `CombinedAutocompleteProvider` (slash commands, prompt templates, extension commands, skill commands, file path completion using `fd`).
- Image paste writes clipboard image to a temp file and inserts the file path into the editor.

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `src/interactive.rs` reserves Tab for future autocomplete but has no autocomplete UI.
- No `@` file fuzzy search.
- No Shift+Enter newline semantics (current behavior differs).
- No `!` / `!!` bash shortcuts.
- No clipboard image paste handler.

# Scope / Deliverables
## 1) Autocomplete engine
- Provide suggestions for:
  - Slash commands (built-in)
  - Prompt templates (`/<template>`)
  - Skill commands (`/skill:name`) when enabled
  - File references after typing `@` (fuzzy search project files)
  - Path completion when cursor is inside a path token

## 2) Autocomplete UI
- Render a dropdown/list with:
  - configurable max visible items (`autocomplete_max_visible`)
  - keyboard navigation
  - selection/accept behavior

## 3) Multi-line input semantics
- Shift+Enter inserts newline; Enter submits.
- Windows Terminal: Ctrl+Enter inserts newline.

## 4) Bash shortcuts
- `!cmd`: run and include output in LLM context.
- `!!cmd`: run but do not include output in LLM context.

## 5) Clipboard image paste and drag/drop
- Ctrl+V attempts to read clipboard image:
  - if an image exists, write it to a temp file and insert the file path at cursor.
  - do nothing (silent) if clipboard has no image or access denied.
- Drag/drop: terminals usually paste a file path; ensure we handle file paths cleanly.

# Testing
- Unit tests for autocomplete matching and insertion.
- State tests for key interactions (Tab, Shift+Enter, Ctrl+V).
- Integration tests for `!` / `!!` behavior (using deterministic command runner where possible).

# Dependencies
- Keybinding routing (`bd-3ip`) for Tab / Shift+Enter / Ctrl+V behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] Autocomplete exists (commands/templates/skills/files/paths)
[ ] Tab path completion works
[ ] @ fuzzy file reference works
[ ] Shift+Enter inserts newline; Enter submits
[ ] ! and !! bash shortcuts implemented
[ ] Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment supported
[ ] Editor settings are wired (padding, max visible)
